messages.content: You are an expert EU proposal writer for IRIS Technology Solutions, a photonics and NIR spectroscopy company. Write in first-person plural (we/our). Use precise technical language. Follow EU Horizon proposal conventions: numbered tasks (**Task X.Y**), clear objectives, evidence-based claims. Present the work plan with work packages, tasks, deliverables, milestones, and Gantt logic.
Write the Work plan and work packages section for a Horizon Europe proposal.

3.1  Work plan and resources   
In line with the scientific and technological objectives of RECREATE, our work in this 48-month project has been 
structured in 9 work packages (WP, Fig.16), each sub-divided into a number of dedicated tasks covering the activities 
planned for our project, as listed in detail in Table 4. We will have Prof. G. Griffini, associate 
professor in Materials Science and Technology at POLIMI, as our project coordinator. Each WP will be led and managed by a project partner 
who is expert in the relevant field, as described in detail in Section 3.2. This WP structure aims to set the basis for a 
rational and seamless execution of our project activities and successfully achieve the foreseen objectives, implemented 
in a reasonable and cost-effective manner. The scientific, technological, techno-economical, environmental and 
educational challenges addressed in RECREATE are in line with the HORIZON-CL4-2021-RESILIENCE-01-01 
topic. Active collaboration between all 21 partners will generate new scientific knowledge, develop novel, sustainable 
and economically profitable technologies, and implement novel approaches to learning and education in the field of 
composite circularity.  
  
   
Fig.  
16 
  
WP interconnections and workflow.

You are an expert EU proposal writer for IRIS Technology Solutions, a photonics and NIR spectroscopy company. Write in first-person plural (we/our). Use precise technical language. Follow EU Horizon proposal conventions: numbered tasks (**Task X.Y**), clear objectives, evidence-based claims. Present the work plan with work packages, tasks, deliverables, milestones, and Gantt logic.
Write the Work plan and work packages section for a Horizon Europe proposal.

3.1 Quality and motivation of the team:  IRIS, founded in Barcelona in 2007, is a leading European manufacturer and integrator of photonics and AI solutions for real-time monitoring and control of industrial processes. We specialise in NIR Spectroscopy, Hyperspectral Imaging, Raman, LIBS, and other techniques (e.g. UV, fluorescence) and Machine Vision integrating them with AI-driven software deep learning systems to deliver advanced analysis solutions, applied across diverse materials and in complex industrial scenarios, including food, plastics, pharmaceuticals, chemicals, and waste.

Mission: To bring Innovation to Industry by the creation and integration of high-end technologies and solutions.
Vision: To become a world leading manufacturer of photonics and AI solutions by dedicating our passion and talent to push the limits of technology. Our R&D Vision is to use our R&D+I capabilities and R&D projects to capitalise the knowledge, to create new products to the market and to develop new industrial applications, to keep our competitiveness up to date and open new markets.
Our team consists of 70 multidisciplinary experts from the scientific and technological fields required for Industry 4.0 solutions development: programmers, engineers (optical, mechatronic, electronic & process engineers), chemists, physicists, mathematicians, data scientists & technologists. We have an experienced Senior Management Team that leads an organisation structure consisting of Departments (Science; Engineering; Digital; Production; Sales & Marketing; Finance, HR and Admin) each with their own Departmental Manager. We are committed to quality and continuous improvement, and we are certified in ISO 9001:2008 and UNE-EN-ISO 166002: Management Systems R&D&I certified. Our company has two divisions: IRIS Commercial, delivering B2B solutions and services to industry (serving a growing client base of blue chips and mid-caps from the food processing, chemical, pharmaceutical, biobased and process industries); and, IRIS Innovation Lab that works in an Open Innovation context to build new knowledge, knowhow and IP to fuel our innovation pipeline so that we can offer cutting edge solutions to industry via our Commercial Division. We are the developers and manufacturers of the VisumTM range of fully certified CE mark non-destructive, accurate and real-time analytical devices: Visum NIR In-Line™- In-line NIR analyser; Visum Palm™-Handheld NIR analyser; Visum HSI™-Hyperspectral Imaging System; Visum Raman In-Line™-Raman analyser; Visum DeepSight™ - Machine Vision System.
Alongside our technical strengths, IRIS has built strong business and commercial capabilities to support our market-driven innovation strategy. Our team includes experts in business development, market analysis, finance, and investment planning. We have successfully secured and managed public and private funding (national and EU) and work closely with financial advisors and technology transfer experts to develop robust business models for our high-tech products. Our commercial leadership brings experience in enterprise sales, B2B pricing (hardware, services, SaaS), and go-to-market strategy, driving traction through a global network of 19 distributors in 27 countries (and future OEM partnerships). We are proud to have secured the trust of important clients, among them AstraZeneca, Sanofi, Almirall, P&G, LyondellBasell, Europastry, Bimbo, Sonae, General Mills, Reig Jofre, Ecoiberia, etc. In this project, we will develop an investment-ready business plan, including cost-benefit analysis, ROI projections & risk assessment. IRIS has worked with Horizon Results Booster & received commercialisation support from Accio & ICEX and will continue leveraging external expertise to strengthen our capital readiness.

You are an expert EU proposal writer for IRIS Technology Solutions, a photonics and NIR spectroscopy company. Write in first-person plural (we/our). Use precise technical language. Follow EU Horizon proposal conventions: numbered tasks (**Task X.Y**), clear objectives, evidence-based claims. Present the work plan with work packages, tasks, deliverables, milestones, and Gantt logic.
Write the Work plan and work packages section for a Horizon Europe proposal.

3.1 Work plan and resources

Brief presentation of the overall structure of our work plan

Our project work plan is structured into six interlinked Work Packages (WPs) that ensure a coherent progression from sensor technology and multimodal integration platform scale-up (Track 1- WP1, WP2), through implementing multimodal integration in 3 complex industrial use cases for real-world validation (Track 2, WP4), to preparing the frameworks for post-project industrialisation, standardisation, IP protection, business scenarios and exploitation pathways for market deployment and adoption (Track 3, WP4). The design reflects both our scientific/technical ambition, industrial validation, and exploitation-driven objectives.  
Our work plan is also supported with a solid campaign for communication, dissemination and ecosystem networking and engagement (WP5), and robust R&I Project Management (WP6). Our work plan Pert is provided in Fig 10, and the Gantt (Table 15) shows our work plan mapped over 36 months with a phased but overlapping structure that ensures continuous feedback between technical development, integration, validation, and exploitation.

You are an expert EU proposal writer for IRIS Technology Solutions, a photonics and NIR spectroscopy company. Write in first-person plural (we/our). Use precise technical language. Follow EU Horizon proposal conventions: numbered tasks (**Task X.Y**), clear objectives, evidence-based claims. Present the work plan with work packages, tasks, deliverables, milestones, and Gantt logic.
Write the Work plan and work packages section for a Horizon Europe proposal.

3.1 Work plan and resources

Brief presentation of the overall structure of our work plan

Our project work plan is structured to deliver a cohesive, cross-disciplinary response to the challenges of carbon-neutral steelmaking. From the 5 research and innovation blocks that underpin the methodology, our work plan has been designed around 8 Work Packages (WPs). It follows a clear technology-maturation and system-integration flow (as presented in the methodology):

Block 1: Bio-anthracite and biogenic hydrogen-rich from biowaste for Low-CO2 steelmaking: We focus on bioanthracite production from biowastes, optimising conversion routes (HTT, leaching, pyrolysis) and scaling them from TRL 4 to TRL 6. We ensure that bioanthracite meets the stringent quality and safety requirements for steelmaking. We target the recovery of green hydrogen from aqueous phases via SCWG/AD, closing the loop on biowaste valorisation while maximising carbon recovery rates. Validation tests at lab and demo scale will be performed in WP3.

Block 2: Circularity by reusing steel waste for new green steel processes: WP3 also integrates circular outputs with steelmaking residues (e.g. slag, metal fines) to create usable reductants and feedstocks, validating these combinations in three production routes: BF–BOF, EAF, and DRI. Additionally, we will study the use of green gas as an alternative to natural gas for use in reheating furnaces.

Block 3: Energy integration and efficiency: WP4 addresses energy integration across steelmaking and biorefinery units, exploring reuse of EAF waste heat to power bioconversion units, use of pyrogas for electricity generation, and improving the energy balance of the entire process chain.

Block 4: Digitalisation and process intelligence: WP5 develops HoloSteel, a digital intelligence layer integrating Process Analytical Technologies (PAT), Quality by Design (QbD), real-time monitoring, and predictive simulation. This ensures stable operation and decision support under variable input conditions. WP5 also ensures data interoperability with the DIGITAL-61 initiative and provides digital feedback to upstream and downstream WPs.

Block 5: Industry 5.0, business case, and ecosystem engagement: WP6 anchors the human and organisational transformation, co-developing operational roles, training content, ESG strategies, and business models with industrial stakeholders. WP7 leads outreach, standardisation, clustering, and impact valorisation, maximising alignment with EU missions (e.g. Clean Steel Partnership, Net-Zero Industry Act) and ensuring that key exploitable results feed into industrial deployment and policy.

WP4 cuts across all blocks (and particularly Block 3), providing a comprehensive impact assessment (LCA, LCC, social impact, risk analysis), benchmarking our project’s value chain against conventional steelmaking processes and supporting informed scale-up.

WP8 ensures coordination, consortium management, and alignment with cross-project EU frameworks. This plan is built to maximise synergy across disciplines (materials, digital, energy, and social sciences) and ensure tight cross-WP integration via shared milestones and data feedback loops, e.g. pilot data from WP3 feeds into both the HoloSteel refinement in WP5 & training design in WP6, while the impact analysis in WP4 informs business strategy (WP6) and policy dialogue (WP7).
